title: CIL Electrical — Full-length Mock 01
pattern: CIL MT — 200 MCQ · 2 papers · 1 mark each · no negative marking

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: As per Income Tax Department data (June 2026), India's net direct tax collections in early FY27 rose by about what percentage year-on-year?
questions.solution: Net direct tax collections rose ~14.6% YoY to about Rs 5.21 trillion.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: In 2026, which country became the first Central Asian member of the New Development Bank (NDB)?
questions.solution: Uzbekistan became the first Central Asian nation to join the BRICS-backed NDB.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Which India-born theoretical physicist won the 2025 Wolf Prize in Physics?
questions.solution: Rajasthan-born physicist Jainendra K. Jain received the 2025 Wolf Prize in Physics.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Which institution ranked first in the QS World University Rankings 2027 (released June 2026)?
questions.solution: Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT) ranked first in QS World University Rankings 2027.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: In June 2026, which Indian jurist was elected a Judge of the International Tribunal for the Law of the Sea (ITLOS)?
questions.solution: Indian jurist Bimal N. Patel was elected to ITLOS in June 2026.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: In 2026, PM Narendra Modi received the highest national award of which country?
questions.solution: PM Modi was conferred Slovakia's highest national award in 2026.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Exercise KHAAN QUEST 2026, in which an Indian Army contingent participated, was held in which country?
questions.solution: The multinational Exercise KHAAN QUEST 2026 was held in Mongolia.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Who was named the new Chairman of GIC Re in June 2026?
questions.solution: The government named Hitesh Joshi as the new Chairman of GIC Re.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: In 2026, ONGC was assigned to develop a new strategic petroleum reserve (SPR) facility at which location?
questions.solution: ONGC was tasked with building a new underground crude oil SPR cavern at Mangaluru, Karnataka.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: The India–UK CETA and Social Security Agreement were reported to take effect from which date?
questions.solution: Reports indicated the India–UK CETA would take effect from July 15, 2026.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: The headquarters of Coal India Limited (CIL) is located in which city?
questions.solution: Coal India Limited is headquartered in Kolkata, West Bengal.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Which is the largest coal-producing state in India in recent years?
questions.solution: Chhattisgarh has been India's largest coal-producing state, ahead of Odisha and Jharkhand.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Coal India Limited operates under which Union Ministry?
questions.solution: CIL functions under the Ministry of Coal, Government of India.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Which subsidiary of CIL is the single largest coal-producing company?
questions.solution: South Eastern Coalfields Limited (SECL) is among CIL's largest producing subsidiaries.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: What is the capital of Australia?
questions.solution: Canberra is the capital of Australia, not Sydney or Melbourne.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: What is the capital of Canada?
questions.solution: Ottawa is the national capital of Canada.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Mount Kilimanjaro, the highest mountain in Africa, is located in which country?
questions.solution: Mount Kilimanjaro lies in north-eastern Tanzania.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Which is the largest planet in the Solar System?
questions.solution: Jupiter is the largest planet by both mass and diameter.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Which planet is known as the Red Planet?
questions.solution: Iron oxide on its surface gives Mars a reddish appearance.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: The SI unit of electric current is the:
questions.solution: The ampere (A) is the SI base unit of electric current.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Which gas is most abundant in Earth's atmosphere?
questions.solution: Nitrogen makes up about 78% of the atmosphere by volume.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: The powerhouse of the cell is the:
questions.solution: Mitochondria generate most of the cell's ATP, earning the 'powerhouse' name.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: What is the chemical symbol of Gold?
questions.solution: Gold's symbol is Au (from Latin 'aurum'); Ag is silver.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: What is the chemical symbol of Sodium?
questions.solution: Sodium's symbol is Na (from Latin 'natrium'); Sn is tin.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: The most abundant metal in the Earth's crust is:
questions.solution: Aluminium is the most abundant metal in the Earth's crust.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A trader marks goods 40% above cost, then gives a 25% discount. His net profit percentage is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Net selling factor is the markup factor times the discount factor; profit% is that factor reduced by 1.
**Formula.** $f=\left(1+\tfrac{m}{100}\right)\left(1-\tfrac{d}{100}\right)$
**Given.** Markup m = 40%, discount d = 25%.
**Working.** $f=\left(1+\tfrac{40}{100}\right)\left(1-\tfrac{25}{100}\right)=1.05$.
**Answer.** Profit $=(f-1)\times100=5\%$.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: The angle between the hour and minute hands of a clock at 7:48 is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The hour hand sweeps 30° per hour and 0.5° per minute, the minute hand 6° per minute; take the smaller of the two angles.
**Formula.** $\theta=\left|30H-5.5M\right|$
**Given.** H = 7, M = 48.
**Working.** $=\left|30\times7-5.5\times48\right|=54^\circ$; smaller $=\min(54,360-54)$.
**Answer.** $\theta=54^\circ$.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A pipe fills a tank in 4 h, but with a leak it takes 5 h. The leak alone can empty the full tank in:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The leak's rate is the fill-rate without the leak minus the slower fill-rate with the leak.
**Formula.** $T=\tfrac{ab}{b-a}$
**Given.** Fill without leak a = 4 h; with leak b = 5 h.
**Working.** Leak rate $=\tfrac1{4}-\tfrac1{5}$; emptying time $=\tfrac{4\times5}{5-4}$.
**Answer.** $T=20$ h.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A invests Rs 4000 for 8 months and B invests Rs 8000 for 4 months. Out of a total profit of Rs 3000, A's share is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Profit is split in the ratio of capital × time invested by each partner.
**Formula.** $\text{share}_A=P\cdot\tfrac{x\,m_A}{x\,m_A+y\,m_B}$
**Given.** A: Rs 4000×8 mo; B: Rs 8000×4 mo; profit Rs 3000.
**Working.** Ratio $=32000:32000$; share$_A=3000\times\tfrac{32000}{64000}$.
**Answer.** A's share $=$ Rs 1500.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A boat covers 65 km downstream in 5 h and the same 65 km upstream in 13 h. Its speed in still water is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Downstream speed adds the stream; upstream subtracts it. Still-water speed is the average of the two.
**Formula.** $u=\tfrac12\left[(b+s)+(b-s)\right]$
**Given.** Downstream 65 km in 5 h; upstream 65 km in 13 h.
**Working.** Downstream $=\tfrac{65}{5}=13$ km/h, upstream $=\tfrac{65}{13}=5$ km/h.
**Answer.** Still-water speed $u=\tfrac{13+5}{2}=9$ km/h.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: The difference between compound and simple interest on Rs 10000 at 12% per annum for 2 years is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Over 2 years the gap between compound and simple interest is the first year's interest-on-interest.
**Formula.** $CI-SI=P\left(\tfrac{r}{100}\right)^2$
**Given.** P = Rs 10000, r = 12% p.a., t = 2 yr.
**Working.** $=10000\left(\tfrac{12}{100}\right)^2$.
**Answer.** $CI-SI=$ Rs 144.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: 4 kg of rice at Rs 30/kg is mixed with 6 kg at Rs 80/kg. The average price per kg is:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: If 12 men complete a work in 9 days, 6 men will complete it in:
questions.solution: Men-days constant: 12×9 = 6×D, D = 18 days.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: The LCM of 12 and 15 is:
questions.solution: The lowest common multiple of 12 and 15 is 60.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: An item bought for Rs 600 is sold for Rs 750. The profit percentage is:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A man goes at 60 km/h and returns over the same route at 60 km/h. His average speed is:
questions.solution: Average speed = 2uv/(u+v) = 2×60×60/(60+60) = 60 km/h.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A car covers a distance at 72 km/h in 6 hours. The distance is:
questions.solution: Distance = speed × time = 72 × 6 = 432 km.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A sum doubles itself in 20 years under simple interest. The annual rate is:
questions.solution: To double, SI = P, so rate = 100/T = 100/20 = 5%.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: The percentage increase when a value rises from 80 to 92 is:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: Successive discounts of 15% and 10% on a marked price of Rs 1000 give a final price of:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A 150 m long train crosses a pole in 15 seconds. Its speed is:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: If 40% of a number is 90, the number is:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: The HCF of 54 and 72 is:
questions.solution: The highest common factor of 54 and 72 is 18.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: If 121 is divided in the ratio 4 : 7, the larger part is:
questions.solution: Larger part = 121 × 7/11 = 77.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A and B invest in the ratio 4 : 5. Out of a total profit of Rs 9000, A's share is:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: The probability of getting an even number on a single throw of a fair die is:
questions.solution: Count the favourable outcomes out of 6 equally likely faces to get 1/2.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: Pipe A fills a tank in 10 h and pipe B in 5 h. Together they fill it in:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A number divided by 8 gives quotient 13 and remainder 6. The number is:
questions.solution: Number = divisor × quotient + remainder = 8×13+6 = 110.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: Compound interest on Rs 15000 at 5% per annum for 2 years is:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: The square root of 4225 is:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: A man walks 3 km towards north, then turns east and walks 4 km. How far is he from the starting point?
questions.solution: Straight-line distance = √(3² + 4²) = √25 = 5 km.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: In a certain code, each letter of a word is moved two steps forward in the English alphabet. The code for 'PEN' is:
questions.solution: Replace every letter by the one two places ahead: PEN → RGP.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: X is the husband of Y. Z is the mother of Y. How is Z related to X?
questions.solution: Tracing each relationship step by step gives: Mother-in-law.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the next term in the series: 3, 7, 15, 31, 63, ?
questions.solution: Each term = previous × 2 + 1, so the next term = 63 × 2 + 1 = 127.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: If A = 1, B = 2, … , Z = 26, what is the sum of the letter values of 'ICE'?
questions.solution: Add the positional values of I, C, E to get 17.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: A person facing north turns 90° to the right. Now they face:
questions.solution: Turning right from north leads to East.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the next term: Z, X, V, T, ?
questions.solution: Letters decrease by 2 each time, so the next letter is R.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the next term: AB, DE, GH, ?
questions.solution: Each pair skips one letter; after GH comes JK.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: If each letter is replaced by the next letter, ROSE is coded as:
questions.solution: Shift each letter forward by one to get SPTF.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: If A=1, B=2, ... then the sum of the letters of ACE is:
questions.solution: Add the alphabet positions of A, C, E to get 9.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: In a row of 18 people, X is 9th from the left. X's position from the right is:
questions.solution: Position from right = 18 − 9 + 1 = 10.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the next term: 2, 6, 18, 54, ?
questions.solution: Each term is multiplied by 3, so next = 54 × 3 = 162.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.solution: Each number is halved, so 20 → 10.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Which is the odd one out: 3, 5, 9, 11?
questions.solution: 3, 5, 11 are prime; 9 is composite.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: All cats are animals. All animals breathe. Which conclusion follows?
questions.solution: Both premises are universal affirmatives, so the chained conclusion 'All cats breathe' follows.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: If 3 January is a Monday, what day is 10 January?
questions.solution: 10 − 3 = 7 days, exactly one week, so it is again Monday.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Which is the odd one out: 64, 125, 200, 216?
questions.solution: The others are perfect cubes; 200 is not.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Which is the odd one out: Apple, Mango, Banana, Potato?
questions.solution: Potato belongs to a different category from the rest.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: The angle between the hands of a clock at 3:00 is:
questions.solution: Each hour = 30°; at 3:00 the hands are 3 × 30 = 90° apart.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the next term: N, O, P, Q, ?
questions.solution: Consecutive alphabet letters; after Q comes R.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the next term: 4, 9, 16, 25, ?
questions.solution: These are perfect squares; next = 6² = 36.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Which of these fractions is the largest?
questions.solution: Comparing decimal values, 4/5 is the largest.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: A man walks 9 km north, then 12 km east. His distance from the start is:
questions.solution: Resultant = √(9² + 12²) = 15 km.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the next term: 4, 6, 10, 16, ?
questions.solution: Differences grow by 2; adding the next difference gives 24.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the synonym of 'Abundant'.
questions.solution: 'Abundant' means existing in large quantity, i.e. plentiful.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the synonym of 'Brief'.
questions.solution: 'Brief' means short or concise.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the synonym of 'Happy'.
questions.solution: 'Happy' is synonymous with 'joyful'.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the synonym of 'Enormous'.
questions.solution: 'Enormous' means very large, i.e. huge.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the synonym of 'Diligent'.
questions.solution: 'Diligent' describes someone hard-working and careful.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the synonym of 'Candid'.
questions.solution: 'Candid' means open and frank in speech.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the synonym of 'Vivid'.
questions.solution: 'Vivid' means bright, clear and intense.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the antonym of 'Ancient'.
questions.solution: 'Ancient' (very old) is opposite to 'modern'.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the antonym of 'Victory'.
questions.solution: 'Victory' is opposite to 'defeat'.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the antonym of 'Expand'.
questions.solution: 'Expand' is opposite to 'contract'.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the antonym of 'Generous'.
questions.solution: 'Generous' is opposite to 'stingy' (mean with money).

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the antonym of 'Transparent'.
questions.solution: 'Transparent' (see-through) is opposite to 'opaque'.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the antonym of 'Optimist'.
questions.solution: An optimist's opposite is a 'pessimist'.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the correctly spelt word.
questions.solution: The correct spelling is 'Occasion' (one c, two s).

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Fill in the blank: 'She is good ___ mathematics.'
questions.solution: The idiom is 'good at' a subject or skill.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Fill in the blank: 'He has been living here ___ 2010.'
questions.solution: 'Since' is used with a point of time like a year.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Fill in the blank: 'Divide this ___ two parts.'
questions.solution: 'Divide into' is the correct collocation for separation.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Fill in the blank: 'He is married ___ a doctor.'
questions.solution: The correct collocation is 'married to' someone.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Fill in the blank: 'The book is ___ the table.'
questions.solution: 'On' indicates resting on a surface.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: One word for 'a person who cannot read or write':
questions.solution: Such a person is 'illiterate'.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The study of living organisms is called:
questions.solution: 'Biology' is the study of all living organisms.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: One who studies stars and celestial bodies is an:
questions.solution: An 'astronomer' scientifically studies celestial bodies.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: One word for 'a speech made without preparation':
questions.solution: An unprepared speech is delivered 'extempore'.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: A place where birds are kept is called:
questions.solution: An 'aviary' houses birds; an apiary keeps bees.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The idiom 'to break the ice' means:
questions.solution: 'Break the ice' means to initiate conversation in a tense or new setting.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The back EMF of a DC motor is:
questions.solution: Eb = (PφZN)/(60A); it rises with speed and flux and opposes the supply.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In an induction motor, the rotor copper loss equals:
questions.solution: Of the air-gap power Pag, a fraction s is dissipated as rotor copper loss and (1 − s) becomes mechanical power.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A series circuit has R = 10 Ω, XL = 20 Ω, XC = 12 Ω across 200 V. The current is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In the two-wattmeter method on a balanced 3-phase load, the two readings are equal when the power factor is:
questions.solution: At unity pf the phase angle is zero and both wattmeters read equal positive power; at pf 0.5 one reads zero.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A sinusoidal voltage has a peak value of 325 V. Its RMS value is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In a purely capacitive AC circuit, the current:
options: Is in phase with the voltage
questions.solution: An ideal capacitor makes current lead voltage by 90°.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An auto-transformer is more economical than a two-winding transformer mainly because:
options: Part of the power is transferred conductively, saving copper
questions.solution: In an auto-transformer only part of the power is transformed inductively; the conductive path reduces copper and size, best for ratios near 1.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A 60 V source drives a 4 Ω resistor. The current is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The form factor of a sinusoidal waveform is:
questions.solution: Form factor = RMS/average (half cycle) = 0.707/0.637 ≈ 1.11.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A copper wire (ρ = 1.7×10⁻⁸ Ω·m) is 100 m long with 2 mm² cross-section. Its resistance is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Kirchhoff's Voltage Law (KVL) is a statement of the conservation of:
questions.solution: The sum of voltage drops around a closed loop equals zero — energy supplied equals energy absorbed.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The skin effect in an AC conductor causes:
options: Current to crowd at the centre
Current to crowd toward the surface, raising effective resistance
questions.solution: At higher frequency current concentrates near the surface, increasing the effective AC resistance.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A current of 3 A flows through a 5 Ω resistor. The power dissipated is:
questions.solution: P = I²R = 3²×5 = 45 W.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Transformers are rated in kVA rather than kW because:
options: Their losses depend on voltage and current, not on the load power factor
questions.solution: Iron loss depends on voltage and copper loss on current; both are independent of the load power factor, so apparent power (kVA) is the rating.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A series circuit has L = 0.2 H and C = 100 µF. Its resonant frequency is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Total power in a balanced 3-phase load is:
questions.solution: P = √3·VL·IL·cos φ in terms of line quantities.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The energy stored in an inductor carrying current I is:
questions.solution: Energy = ½LI², stored in the magnetic field of the coil.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The balanced Wheatstone bridge shown has P = 10 Ω, Q = 50 Ω and S = 40 Ω (galvanometer reads zero). The unknown resistance R is:
questions.solution: At balance P/Q = R/S, so R = P·S/Q = 10×40/50 = 8 Ω.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Electrical power is transmitted at high voltage mainly to:
questions.solution: For a given power, higher voltage means lower current, so I²R losses fall.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A series RLC circuit has R = 20 Ω, L = 0.2 H, C = 40 µF. Its quality factor Q is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Adding a pole to the open-loop transfer function generally:
options: Reduces stability and makes the response more oscillatory
questions.solution: An extra pole adds phase lag, pushing the root locus toward the right half-plane and reducing stability margins.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Two resistors of 4 Ω and 6 Ω in series give a total resistance of:
questions.solution: Series resistances simply add: 4 + 6 = 10 Ω.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In the divider shown (24 V applied), the output voltage Vout across R2 is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In a purely inductive AC circuit, the current:
options: Is in phase with the voltage
questions.solution: An ideal inductor makes current lag voltage by 90°.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The current-versus-frequency curve of a series RLC circuit peaks at the marked frequency f₀ ≈ 50 Hz. At this resonant frequency the power factor is:
questions.solution: At series resonance XL = XC, the impedance is purely resistive and current peaks, so the power factor is unity. (Here f₀ ≈ 50 Hz.)

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 130" width="300" height="130"><line x1="20" y1="110" x2="290" y2="110" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1"/><line x1="20" y1="15" x2="20" y2="110" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1"/><path d="M20 108 C 110 105 140 20 160 20 C 180 20 210 105 290 108" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="160" y1="20" x2="160" y2="110" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><text x="160" y="124" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#dc2626">f₀</text><text x="26" y="20" font-size="10" fill="#475569">I</text><text x="278" y="124" font-size="10" fill="#475569">f</text></svg>
figure.caption: Series-resonance current curve

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The superposition theorem is applicable only to:
questions.solution: Superposition holds for linear, bilateral networks; responses of independent sources add.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Power-factor improvement of an inductive load is usually done by connecting:
questions.solution: A shunt capacitor supplies leading reactive power, raising the lagging power factor.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A balanced 3-phase load draws 20 A at 415 V line, pf 0.9. The total power is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For a reciprocal two-port described by ABCD parameters, the condition satisfied is:
questions.solution: Reciprocity of a passive two-port gives the determinant AD − BC = 1.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The impedance triangle shown has R = 3 Ω and X = 6 Ω. The impedance phase angle is about:

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 200 140" width="200" height="140"><line x1="20" y1="120" x2="180" y2="120" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1"/><line x1="20" y1="120" x2="20" y2="10" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1"/><line x1="20" y1="120" x2="140" y2="120" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="2.5"/><text x="90" y="134" font-size="10" fill="#16a34a">R=3Ω</text><line x1="140" y1="120" x2="140" y2="50" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="2.5"/><text x="145" y="85" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">X=6Ω</text><line x1="20" y1="120" x2="140" y2="50" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="2.5"/><text x="70" y="78" font-size="10" fill="#2563eb">Z</text></svg>
figure.caption: Impedance triangle (R–X)

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Kirchhoff's Current Law (KCL) is a statement of the conservation of:
questions.solution: KCL says the algebraic sum of currents at a node is zero — charge is neither created nor destroyed.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The synchronous speed of an AC machine with P poles at frequency f is:
questions.solution: Ns = 120f/P rpm.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The impedance triangle shown has R = 4 Ω and X = 6 Ω. The impedance phase angle is about:

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 200 140" width="200" height="140"><line x1="20" y1="120" x2="180" y2="120" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1"/><line x1="20" y1="120" x2="20" y2="10" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1"/><line x1="20" y1="120" x2="140" y2="120" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="2.5"/><text x="90" y="134" font-size="10" fill="#16a34a">R=4Ω</text><line x1="140" y1="120" x2="140" y2="50" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="2.5"/><text x="145" y="85" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">X=6Ω</text><line x1="20" y1="120" x2="140" y2="50" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="2.5"/><text x="70" y="78" font-size="10" fill="#2563eb">Z</text></svg>
figure.caption: Impedance triangle (R–X)

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The efficiency of a transformer is maximum when:
questions.solution: Maximum efficiency occurs at the load where variable copper loss = constant iron loss.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For the series RLC circuit shown (L = 0.1 H, C = 40 µF), the resonant frequency is about:
questions.solution: At resonance XL = XC, so f₀ = 1/(2π√(LC)) = 1/(2π√(0.1×40µF)) ≈ 79.6 Hz.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 90" width="300" height="90"><line x1="10" y1="45" x2="40" y2="45" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><rect x="40" y="37" width="34" height="16" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="2"/><text x="57" y="32" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#2563eb">R</text><path d="M74 45 q8 -12 16 0 q8 -12 16 0 q8 -12 16 0" fill="none" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="2"/><text x="98" y="30" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#16a34a">L=0.1H</text><line x1="122" y1="45" x2="150" y2="45" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="150" y1="35" x2="150" y2="55" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="158" y1="35" x2="158" y2="55" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="2"/><text x="154" y="30" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#dc2626">C=40µF</text><line x1="158" y1="45" x2="290" y2="45" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="290" y1="45" x2="290" y2="75" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="10" y1="45" x2="10" y2="75" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="10" y1="75" x2="290" y2="75" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><circle cx="150" cy="75" r="3" fill="#1e293b"/><text x="150" y="88" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#475569">~ AC source</text></svg>

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The holding current of a thyristor is:
options: The current needed to latch it on
The minimum anode current to keep it conducting after the gate is removed
questions.solution: Holding current keeps an already-on SCR conducting; latching current (larger) is needed to turn it on.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The Routh–Hurwitz criterion is used to determine:
options: The number of closed-loop poles in the right half-plane
questions.solution: Sign changes in the first column of the Routh array count the RHP poles (unstable roots).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A balanced 3-phase load draws 20 A at 400 V line, pf 0.85. The total power is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The V-curve of a synchronous motor is a plot of:
options: Armature current versus field current at constant load
questions.solution: Varying field excitation at fixed load traces a V-shaped armature-current curve, minimum at unity pf.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In the two-wattmeter method the readings are W₁ = 1200 W and W₂ = 300 W. The total 3-phase power is:
questions.solution: Total power = W₁ + W₂ = 1200 + 300 = 1500 W (the difference relates to reactive power).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The Ferranti effect on a long, lightly loaded transmission line means:
questions.solution: Line capacitance draws leading current that raises the receiving-end voltage above the sending end under light load.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In a balanced star connection, the line voltage is related to the phase voltage by:
questions.solution: In star, line voltage leads phase voltage and VL = √3·Vph; line and phase currents are equal.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The synchronous speed of a 8-pole machine on a 50 Hz supply is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For an ideal buck (step-down) DC–DC converter in continuous conduction, the output voltage is:
questions.solution: A buck converter gives Vo = D·Vs; the boost converter gives Vs/(1−D).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The sinusoidal voltage shown has a peak of 100 V (marked). Its RMS value is about:

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 120" width="300" height="120"><line x1="10" y1="60" x2="290" y2="60" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1"/><line x1="20" y1="10" x2="20" y2="110" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1"/><path d="M20 60 Q 55 0 90 60 T 160 60 T 230 60 T 300 60" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="55" y1="60" x2="55" y2="10" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><text x="60" y="14" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">Vm=100V</text><text x="270" y="74" font-size="10" fill="#475569">t</text></svg>
figure.caption: Sinusoidal voltage waveform

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A wattmeter reads zero on a purely reactive (inductive or capacitive) load because:
options: The average of v·i over a cycle is zero
questions.solution: Pure reactance gives a 90° phase shift; instantaneous power averages to zero over a cycle, so real power is zero.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The RMS value of a sinusoidal voltage of peak value Vm is:
questions.solution: For a sine wave, RMS = Vm/√2 ≈ 0.707 Vm.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Two resistors of 8 Ω and 2 Ω are connected in series. The equivalent resistance is:
questions.solution: Series adds: 8 + 2 = 10 Ω.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A step-up transformer has:
options: More secondary turns than primary turns
questions.solution: Step-up means N₂ > N₁, so V₂ > V₁.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A coil has resistance 3 Ω and reactance 12 Ω. Its impedance magnitude is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A 3-phase synchronous motor is:
questions.solution: It produces no average starting torque and needs auxiliary (e.g. damper-winding) starting.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An over-excited synchronous motor draws a current that is:
questions.solution: Over-excitation makes it draw leading current — used for power-factor correction (synchronous condenser).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An induction motor has air-gap power 30 kW and slip 0.05. The rotor copper loss is:
questions.solution: Rotor copper loss = s × Pag = 0.05 × 30 kW = 1500 W.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Tellegen's theorem is based fundamentally on:
questions.solution: Tellegen's theorem holds for any lumped network satisfying KVL/KCL, regardless of element nature.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For a load with R = 8 Ω and reactance X = 6 Ω, the power factor is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The commutator in a DC machine acts as a:
questions.solution: It mechanically reverses armature connections so the external current/EMF stays unidirectional.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A PMMC (permanent-magnet moving-coil) instrument can directly measure:
questions.solution: PMMC responds to average value; on AC the average is zero, so it reads DC directly (needs a rectifier for AC).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A DC motor on 230 V draws 15 A with armature resistance 0.8 Ω. The back EMF is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The resonant frequency of a series RLC circuit is given by:
questions.solution: At resonance XL = XC, giving f₀ = 1/(2π√(LC)).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The sinusoidal voltage shown has a peak of 200 V (marked). Its RMS value is about:

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 120" width="300" height="120"><line x1="10" y1="60" x2="290" y2="60" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1"/><line x1="20" y1="10" x2="20" y2="110" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1"/><path d="M20 60 Q 55 0 90 60 T 160 60 T 230 60 T 300 60" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="55" y1="60" x2="55" y2="10" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><text x="60" y="14" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">Vm=200V</text><text x="270" y="74" font-size="10" fill="#475569">t</text></svg>
figure.caption: Sinusoidal voltage waveform

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Thevenin's theorem replaces a linear network by:
options: A current source in parallel with a resistance
A voltage source in series with a resistance
questions.solution: Thevenin: an equivalent EMF Vth in series with Rth seen from the terminals.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For maximum power transfer to a complex load ZL from a source of impedance Zth, ZL must equal:
questions.solution: With a reactive source, maximum power needs ZL = Zth* so the reactances cancel and resistances match.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A 6-pole, 50 Hz induction motor runs at 940 rpm. Its slip is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An induction motor develops maximum torque when the rotor resistance per phase equals:
options: The standstill rotor reactance times the slip (R₂ = sX₂)
questions.solution: Maximum torque occurs at the slip where R₂ = sX₂; the peak torque itself is independent of R₂.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For a load with R = 9 Ω and reactance X = 6 Ω, the power factor is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: If one wattmeter in the two-wattmeter method reads zero, the load power factor is:
questions.solution: W₂ = VLIL cos(30°+φ) = 0 when 30°+φ = 90°, i.e. φ = 60°, pf = cos60° = 0.5.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In a balanced delta connection, the line current is related to the phase current by:
questions.solution: In delta, line and phase voltages are equal and IL = √3·Iph.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The inductive reactance of a 0.02 H inductor at 50 Hz is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For maximum power transfer to a resistive load from a DC source, the load resistance should equal:
questions.solution: Maximum power is transferred when RL = Rth.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A 24 V source drives a 4 Ω resistor. The current is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The equivalent resistance of two equal resistors R in parallel is:
questions.solution: For two equal parallel resistors, Req = R·R/(R+R) = R/2.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The EMF equation of a transformer is E =
questions.solution: E = 4.44 f N Φm, from the rate of change of sinusoidal flux.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A 6-pole, 50 Hz induction motor runs at 970 rpm. Its slip is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A DC series motor should never be started:
questions.solution: On no load a series motor races to dangerously high speed because flux falls with the small current.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In the divider shown (12 V applied), the output voltage Vout across R2 is:

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 220 150" width="220" height="150"><line x1="30" y1="20" x2="30" y2="50" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><rect x="22" y="50" width="16" height="34" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="2"/><text x="50" y="70" font-size="10" fill="#2563eb">R1=3Ω</text><line x1="30" y1="84" x2="30" y2="100" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><rect x="22" y="100" width="16" height="34" fill="none" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="2"/><text x="50" y="120" font-size="10" fill="#16a34a">R2=8Ω</text><line x1="30" y1="134" x2="30" y2="145" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><circle cx="30" cy="92" r="3" fill="#dc2626"/><text x="40" y="96" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">Vout</text><text x="35" y="16" font-size="10" fill="#475569">12V</text></svg>
figure.caption: Resistive voltage divider

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The average value of a sinusoidal current over one complete cycle is:
questions.solution: Over a full cycle the positive and negative halves cancel, giving zero average.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The energy stored in a capacitor of capacitance C charged to voltage V is:
questions.solution: Energy = ½CV², stored in the electric field between the plates.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The balanced Wheatstone bridge shown has P = 100 Ω, Q = 200 Ω and S = 60 Ω (galvanometer reads zero). The unknown resistance R is:
questions.solution: At balance P/Q = R/S, so R = P·S/Q = 100×60/200 = 30 Ω.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 200 160" width="200" height="160"><polygon points="100,20 180,80 100,140 20,80" fill="none" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="55" y="45" font-size="10" fill="#2563eb">P=100Ω</text><text x="140" y="45" font-size="10" fill="#2563eb">Q=200Ω</text><text x="55" y="120" font-size="10" fill="#16a34a">R=?</text><text x="135" y="120" font-size="10" fill="#16a34a">S=60Ω</text><circle cx="100" cy="80" r="9" fill="none" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="100" y="84" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#dc2626">G</text></svg>
figure.caption: Wheatstone bridge

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For a single line-to-ground fault, the three sequence networks are connected in:
questions.solution: An LG fault connects positive, negative and zero sequence networks in series.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The synchronous speed of a 2-pole machine on a 60 Hz supply is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Norton's theorem replaces a linear network by:
options: A current source in parallel with a resistance
A voltage source in series with a resistance
questions.solution: Norton: a current source IN in parallel with RN (= Rth).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The slip of an induction motor running at synchronous speed would be:
questions.solution: Slip s = (Ns − N)/Ns; at N = Ns, s = 0 (and torque would be zero).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A source has Vth = 20 V and Rth = 2 Ω. The maximum power deliverable to a matched load is:
questions.solution: Pmax = Vth²/(4Rth) = 20²/(4×2) = 50 W (load = Rth, efficiency 50%).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A moving-iron instrument can measure:
questions.solution: Deflection depends on the square of current, so it works on both AC and DC.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For the series RLC circuit shown (L = 0.5 H, C = 10 µF), the resonant frequency is about:
questions.solution: At resonance XL = XC, so f₀ = 1/(2π√(LC)) = 1/(2π√(0.5×10µF)) ≈ 71.2 Hz.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 90" width="300" height="90"><line x1="10" y1="45" x2="40" y2="45" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><rect x="40" y="37" width="34" height="16" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="2"/><text x="57" y="32" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#2563eb">R</text><path d="M74 45 q8 -12 16 0 q8 -12 16 0 q8 -12 16 0" fill="none" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="2"/><text x="98" y="30" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#16a34a">L=0.5H</text><line x1="122" y1="45" x2="150" y2="45" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="150" y1="35" x2="150" y2="55" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="158" y1="35" x2="158" y2="55" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="2"/><text x="154" y="30" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#dc2626">C=10µF</text><line x1="158" y1="45" x2="290" y2="45" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="290" y1="45" x2="290" y2="75" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="10" y1="45" x2="10" y2="75" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="10" y1="75" x2="290" y2="75" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><circle cx="150" cy="75" r="3" fill="#1e293b"/><text x="150" y="88" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#475569">~ AC source</text></svg>
figure.caption: Series RLC circuit

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Power factor is defined as the cosine of the angle between:
questions.solution: Power factor = cos φ, where φ is the phase angle between voltage and current.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Electrical power dissipated in a resistor can be written as:
questions.solution: P = VI = I²R = V²/R for a resistor.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The capacitive reactance of a 50 µF capacitor at 60 Hz is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The time constant of a series RC circuit is:
questions.solution: τ = RC; after one time constant the capacitor charges to ~63% of the final voltage.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The energy stored in a 100 µF capacitor charged to 10 V is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: At series resonance, the impedance of an RLC circuit is:
questions.solution: XL and XC cancel, leaving only R, so impedance is minimum and resistive (current maximum).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Corona discharge on a transmission line increases with:
questions.solution: Corona worsens as surface gradient rises (higher voltage, thinner or rough conductors, bad weather).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Two resistors of 10 Ω and 12 Ω are connected in parallel. The equivalent resistance is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The time constant of a series RL circuit is:
questions.solution: τ = L/R; the current reaches ~63% of its final value in one time constant.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A transformer with 200 primary and 50 secondary turns is fed 230 V. The secondary voltage is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A 3-phase induction motor at standstill has a slip of:
questions.solution: At start N = 0, so s = (Ns − 0)/Ns = 1.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Ohm's law states that, at constant temperature, the current through a conductor is:
options: Proportional to the square of the voltage
Directly proportional to the voltage across it
questions.solution: V = IR, so for constant R the current rises in direct proportion to the applied voltage.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The balanced Wheatstone bridge shown has P = 20 Ω, Q = 50 Ω and S = 40 Ω (galvanometer reads zero). The unknown resistance R is:
questions.solution: At balance P/Q = R/S, so R = P·S/Q = 20×40/50 = 16 Ω.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 200 160" width="200" height="160"><polygon points="100,20 180,80 100,140 20,80" fill="none" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="55" y="45" font-size="10" fill="#2563eb">P=20Ω</text><text x="140" y="45" font-size="10" fill="#2563eb">Q=50Ω</text><text x="55" y="120" font-size="10" fill="#16a34a">R=?</text><text x="135" y="120" font-size="10" fill="#16a34a">S=40Ω</text><circle cx="100" cy="80" r="9" fill="none" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="100" y="84" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#dc2626">G</text></svg>
figure.caption: Wheatstone bridge

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A transformer operates on the principle of:
questions.solution: Changing flux from the primary links the secondary, inducing an EMF (mutual induction).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The capacitive reactance of a 50 µF capacitor at 50 Hz is about:
